titleEn: International course: ICT tools in scientific and technological production
excerptEn: 23 June to 1 July 2021 With the aim of strengthening the scientific and technological capacities of the Pedro Ruiz Gallo National University (UNPRG), the International Course “ICT Tools in Scientific and Technological Production” will take place from 23 June to 1 July of this year, 2021. The international course will be…
contentEn:
23 June to 1 July 2021

With the aim of strengthening the scientific and technological capacities of the Pedro Ruiz Gallo National University (UNPRG), the International Course “ICT Tools in Scientific and Technological Production” will take place from 23 June to 1 July of this year, 2021.  The international course will be delivered by the teaching researchers Juan Pablo Hernández Ramos, Eva María Torrecilla Sánchez and Fernando Martínez Abad; all three hold doctorates from the University of Salamanca, Spain, awarded with the distinction of Sobresaliente Cum Laude, they are researchers of the GRIAL Group, among others, and they are authors of a range of articles published in journals indexed in Scopus, Web of Science, etc.

The running of this international course falls to the Innovation and Technology Transfer Team of the UNPRG, headed by Dr Hilda Angélica Del Carpio Ramos, who noted that the course has been made possible thanks to the decisive support of Dr Francisco José García Peñalvo, Director of the Research Group in Interaction and e-learning (GRIAL) of the University of Salamanca. She also mentioned that the course is organised into four modules: I) Research: approaches, formulations, designs and aspects relating to planning; II) The scientific article: choosing the journal and structuring the document; III) Instruments for qualitative research; and IV) Instruments for quantitative research. Together they carry a total weight of 2 credits.

The international event will be opened by Dr Olinda Luzmila Vigo Vargas, Rector of the UNPRG, and 300 UNPRG lecturers are expected to take part.

titleEn: Call for papers: International Conference of Research in Education – IRED’21
excerptEn: The challenges of post-pandemic educational research The International Conference of Research in Education (IRED) is a biennial event supported by the Network of Education Research Institutes (RIIE – https://riie.org/). The first edition was held in November 2019 at the University of Barcelona (http://www.ub.edu/ired19/). The second edition of…
contentEn:
The challenges of post-pandemic educational research

The International Conference of Research in Education (IRED) is a biennial event supported by the Network of Education Research Institutes (RIIE – https://riie.org/). The first edition was held in November 2019 at the University of Barcelona (http://www.ub.edu/ired19/).

The second edition of the IRED’21 International Conference will be held at the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE – https://iuce.usal.es/) of the University of Salamanca from 3 to 5 November 2021, in a hybrid format (on-site and online) owing to the uncertainty and the situation arising from the pandemic caused by COVID-19.

The focus is, precisely, the influence of COVID-19 on educational research, with the eye set on the situation in which education will be left once the pandemic is under control and, at best, completely overcome.

Under this motto, this edition of IRED’21 invites papers on the challenges, trends and commitments of research in education looking towards the post-pandemic COVID-19 era. Specifically, though not exclusively, the interest lies in the role of education research institutes, international cooperation in education research, the advantages and limitations of online assessment, and the conception of education through a hybrid lens.

Contributions must be submitted anonymously on the EasyChair platform at https://easychair.org/conferences/?conf=ired21, following the format guidelines set out below. These contributions will be reviewed by the members of the IRED’21 Scientific Committee.

  • Extended abstract with references in APA 7.
  • Title and keywords: in Spanish and English.
  • Maximum length: 2 pages.
  • Style: Arial 10 points, single-spaced.
  • Submission format: PDF.
  • Language: the official language of the conference is Spanish.

Accepted contributions will be published in the conference book of abstracts (with ISBN). A selection of the papers presented will be invited to take part as chapters of the book “The challenges of post-pandemic educational research” (provisional title), with a publisher yet to be decided and indexed in SPI.

Key dates:

  • Submission of papers: 1 July 2021.
  • Notification of acceptance: 19 July 2021.
  • Submission of the final version of the paper: 30 September 2021.

Contact:

  • All questions about submissions should be sent by email to ired2021@usal.es

Further information at https://ired2021.grial.eu/

titleEn: Distinctions for the Administrative and Service Staff of the University of Salamanca
excerptEn: On Monday 14 June, the formal ceremony for the presentation of the distinctions awarded to the Administrative and Service Staff of the University of Salamanca took place at 11:00 in the Paraninfo of the Escuelas Mayores building. In recognition of her professional career, the honoured staff included Yolanda Arias, a member of…
contentEn:
On Monday 14 June, the formal ceremony for the presentation of the distinctions awarded to the Administrative and Service Staff of the University of Salamanca took place at 11:00 in the Paraninfo of the Escuelas Mayores building.

In recognition of her professional career, the honoured staff included Yolanda Arias, a member of the administrative staff of the University Institute of Education Sciences.

From the IUCE we congratulate you and thank you for all these years with us, Yolanda!

titleEn: Offer of Research Manager Positions at the University of Salamanca
excerptEn: With the aim of supporting the promotion and management of research and transfer at the University of Salamanca, the Vice-Rectorate for Research and Transfer is launching the VIIB_2021 RESEARCH MANAGERS PROGRAMME. The VIIB_2021 Programme is divided into two types of contract. The first type comprises contracts for managers who will join…
contentEn:
With the aim of supporting the promotion and management of research and transfer at the University of Salamanca, the Vice-Rectorate for Research and Transfer is launching the VIIB_2021 RESEARCH MANAGERS PROGRAMME.

The VIIB_2021 Programme is divided into two types of contract. The first type comprises contracts for managers who will join departments, institutes and research groups selected in a previous phase of this programme. The second comprises contracts for managers supporting researchers in central units: the Research Management Agency and the International Projects Office.

The published call and the application forms can be found at the following links:

https://sede.usal.es/tablon/anuncio/4349/

https://investigacion.usal.es/es/investigacion/programas-propios

The application deadline is 21 June 2021.

titleEn: The Audiovisual Content Observatory (OCA) recognised as a Consolidated Research Unit (UIC) by the Regional Government of Castilla y León
excerptEn: The OCA, a research group of the University of Salamanca led by Juan José Igartua, Professor of Audiovisual Communication and Advertising at the Faculty of Social Sciences, was recognised, as of 1 June 2021, as a Consolidated Research Unit (UIC) of Castilla y León, with the number 313 assigned to it. The UIC is made up of…
contentEn:
The OCA, a research group of the University of Salamanca led by Juan José Igartua, Professor of Audiovisual Communication and Advertising at the Faculty of Social Sciences, was recognised, as of 1 June 2021, as a Consolidated Research Unit (UIC) of Castilla y León, with the number 313 assigned to it. The UIC is made up of Juan José Igartua Perosanz (director), Félix Ortega Mohedano, Magdalena Wojceiszak (University of California Davis, USA), Carlos Edmundo Arcila Calderón, Beatriz González de Garay Domínguez and María Marcos Ramos.

This recognition is an excellent opportunity to celebrate the 15th anniversary of the creation of the OCA, which was born in 2006 as a Recognised Research Group (GIR) of the University of Salamanca. The research group was constituted as such after winning the First Research Prize in the 6th “Santo Padre Rubio, SJ” Prize (2005) for advances in the knowledge of immigration, awarded by the University Institute for Migration Studies of Comillas Pontifical University. In addition, in 2012, the Directorate-General for Universities and Research of the Regional Ministry of Education recognised the OCA as a Research Group of Excellence of Castilla y León (GR319). Fifteen years after its foundation as a GIR, the OCA has now been recognised as a UIC of Castilla y León.

All of this constitutes recognition of the work carried out by the team that makes up the group, whose leitmotiv has been a commitment to quality, scientific rigour and internationalisation.

This recognition will allow the members of the UIC to access higher-level competitive calls, increasing their capacity to obtain grants, postdoctoral contracts and funded projects.

Link to the source of this news item: https://www.ocausal.es/el-observatorio-de-los-contenidos-audiovisuales-oca-reconocido-como-unidad-de-investigacion-consolidada-de-la-junta-de-castilla-y-leon/

Contact: Dr Juan José Igartua. Director of the Audiovisual Content Observatory. Email: jigartua@usal.es. Telephone: 676.161.042

titleEn: The General Training Programme gets under way with the first FIPU meeting of the 2020/2021 academic year
excerptEn: Today, Friday 30 April, those enrolled on the 2021 Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff (FIPU) met through the Studium virtual platform with the coordinators of the activity, Susana Olmos Miguelánez and Eva Mª Torrecilla Sánchez. The session was introduced by the Vice-Rector for Teaching and Educational Innovation,…
contentEn: Today, Friday 30 April, those enrolled on the 2021 Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff (FIPU) met through the Studium virtual platform with the coordinators of the activity, Susana Olmos Miguelánez and Eva Mª Torrecilla Sánchez. The session was introduced by the Vice-Rector for Teaching and Educational Innovation, Mª José Rodríguez Conde. This activity marks the start of the General Training Programme, which will finish in September but will mostly take place during May and June.

titleEn: The Audiovisual Content Observatory (OCA) awarded the Roblón Prize “Communication Research Group of the Year Award”
excerptEn: The advisory committee of Revista Latina de Comunicación Social, meeting in ordinary session in March 2021, has awarded the 2020 Roblón Prize, in the category Communication Research Group of the Year Award, to the Audiovisual Content Observatory (www.ocausal.es) for its rigour and its contribution to academia and to society as…
contentEn:
The advisory committee of Revista Latina de Comunicación Social, meeting in ordinary session in March 2021, has awarded the 2020 Roblón Prize, in the category Communication Research Group of the Year Award, to the Audiovisual Content Observatory (www.ocausal.es) for its rigour and its contribution to academia and to society as a whole.

The OCA, a Recognised Research Group of the University of Salamanca led by Juan José Igartua, Professor of Audiovisual Communication and Advertising at the Faculty of Social Sciences: “receives this recognition owing to its consolidated track record in the field of empirical studies of communication processes. In particular, the OCA is currently leading the production of experimental research in the field of media effects in Spain, with special emphasis on the relationship between communication and education, which has brought the group growing internationalisation and visibility. Likewise, the OCA leads a novel line of work devoted to applying computational methods and big data analysis techniques to communication research, which makes this research group a pioneer in the application of advanced methodologies in the field. The OCA has a well-coordinated team of high-level researchers that guarantees high-quality academic output, transfer to society and the training of new generations of researchers”.

It is worth noting that the OCA was born in 2005, after winning the First Research Prize in the 6th “Santo Padre Rubio, SJ” Prize for advances in the knowledge of immigration, awarded by the University Institute for Migration Studies of Comillas Pontifical University. Exactly 10 years later, in 2015, the same Prize was awarded once again, highlighting its scientific rigour and its methodological approach, as well as the timeliness of the topic.

On 21 September 2006, the Vice-Rectorate for Research of the University of Salamanca recognised the OCA as a Recognised Research Group. In addition, on 8 October 2012, the Directorate-General for Universities and Research of the Regional Ministry of Education recognised the OCA as a Research Group of Excellence of Castilla y León (GR319). Fifteen years after its foundation as a Recognised Research Group of the University of Salamanca, in 2021, the OCA has submitted its application for recognition as a Consolidated Research Unit of Castilla y León.

The OCA currently has more than 20 researchers, and one of its main research lines is the study of media effects using experimental methodology, an aspect noted in the decision of the Advisory Committee that awarded the Roblón Prize: “the OCA is currently leading the production of experimental research in the field of media effects in Spain”. In this respect, from the perspective of Media Psychology (the psychology of the media), the members of the OCA have published work on media effects in high-impact international journals, most notably the following: “Moderating effect of group cue while processing news on immigration: is framing effect a heuristic process?” (Journal of Communication, 2009), “Identification with characters and narrative persuasion through fictional feature films” (Communications, 2010), “Cognitive, attitudinal and emotional effects of the news frame and group cues on processing news about immigration” (Journal of Media Psychology, 2011), “Changing real-world beliefs with controversial movies: processes and mechanisms of narrative persuasion” (Journal of Communication, 2012), “Enhancing attitudes toward stigmatized groups with movies: mediating and moderating processes of narrative persuasion” (International Journal of Communication, 2017), “How the interplay of imagined contact and first-person narratives improves attitudes toward stigmatized immigrants: a conditional process model” (European Journal of Social Psychology, 2019), “Narrative voice matters! Improving smoking prevention with testimonial messages through identification and cognitive processes” (International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 2020), “The presence of the protagonist: explaining narrative perspective effects through social presence” (Media Psychology, 2020), and “Reduction of prejudice toward unaccompanied foreign minors through audiovisual narratives: effects of the similarity and of the narrative voice” (Profesional de la Información, 2021).

The study of “the relationship between communication and education” is another of the OCA’s research areas that was specially noted in the decision of the Advisory Committee granting the award. In this field, the following works stand out: “Identification with characters, elaboration, and counterarguing in entertainment-education interventions through audiovisual fiction” (Journal of Health Communication, 2016), “Neuromarketing highlights in how Asperger syndrome youth perceive advertising” (Frontiers in Psychology, 2020), “Predicting wellbeing in children’s use of smart screen devices” (Comunicar, 2021) and “Narrative persuasion in a mobile environment:  effectiveness of a mobile application for promoting digital skills and coping strategies in adolescents” (International Journal of Communication, 2021). It should likewise be mentioned that the OCA is one of the main research groups of the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca and is a cornerstone of the Education in the Knowledge Society doctoral programme.

Recently, the OCA has incorporated new and innovative research methodologies based on computational methods and big data techniques applied to the analysis of social media. This aspect was also acknowledged by the Advisory Committee of Revista Latina de Comunicación Social in justifying the award: “the OCA leads a novel line of work devoted to applying computational methods and big data analysis techniques to communication research”. Relevant scientific contributions have also been published in this field, most notably: “Supervised sentiment analysis of science topics: developing a training set of tweets in Spanish” (Journal of Information Technology Research, 2020), “Topic modeling and characterization of hate speech against immigrants on Twitter around the emergence of a far-right party in Spain” (Social Sciences, 2020), “Refugees welcome? Online hate speech and sentiments in twitter in Spain during the reception of the boat Aquarius” (Sustainability, 2021) and “A web interface for analyzing hate speech” (Future Internet, 2021). These works are a direct consequence of the participation of the members of the OCA in international networks and in projects funded by the European Union and other national bodies and institutions, most notably: “Preventing Hate Against Refugees and Migrants (European Commission, Rights, Equality and Citizenship programme)” and “Enhanced migration measures from a multidimensional perspective (European Commission, Horizon 2020 Framework Programme)”.

Finally, a third research area of the OCA is linked to the study of media content and messages. The content analysis studies carried out in this field are equally numerous and have been published in the leading international scientific journals, most notably the following: “The psychosocial portrayals of immigrants in Spanish prime time television fiction (2016-2017)” (Communication and Society, 2019), “Study of the academic production on communication in Spain and Latin America” (Comunicar, 2019), “The gendered representation of refugees using visual frames in the main western European media”(Gender Issues, 2020), “Visual frames of migrants and refugees in the main Western European media” (Economics & Sociology, 2020) and “Gender representation in suscription video-on-demand Spanish TV series” (International Journal of Communiaction, 2021). The prize is an excellent opportunity to celebrate the 15th anniversary of the creation of the OCA as a Recognised Research Group of the University of Salamanca. It likewise constitutes public recognition of the work carried out throughout this time by the team that makes up the group, whose leitmotiv is a commitment to quality, scientific rigour and internationalisation.

Contact: Dr Juan José Igartua. Director of the Audiovisual Content Observatory. Email: jigartua@usal.es. Telephone: 676.161.042

titleEn: Participation of María Serena Rivetta in the “TEACHING, ASSESSMENT AND INNOVATION IN UNIVERSITY” conference
excerptEn: Doctoral candidate Maria Serena Rivetta was invited to take part as a speaker in the round table “What strategies to promote, develop and recognise teaching professionalism in the university system?” at the International Conference “TEACHING, ASSESSMENT AND INNOVATION IN UNIVERSITY”, held on 24 and 25 June 2020, to speak about her thesis…
contentEn:
Doctoral candidate Maria Serena Rivetta was invited to take part as a speaker in the round table “What strategies to promote, develop and recognise teaching professionalism in the university system?” at the International Conference “TEACHING, ASSESSMENT AND INNOVATION IN UNIVERSITY”, held on 24 and 25 June 2020, to speak about her thesis work on university teacher training, supervised by Professors María José Rodriguez Conde and Susana Olmos Migueláñez.

The conference aims to develop a reflection on university teaching, covering the organisational and professional aspects that contribute to creating an inclusive and effective context for the quality of higher education.

Specifically, the work will focus on analysing the models adopted internationally to support the professional quality of university teaching staff, in relation to their significant repercussions on teaching practice and teaching action, concentrating on the relationship between the modernisation of systems, strategic orientations, scientific research and results in the various areas of intervention. Particularly important will be the extended comparison between the different experiences taking shape in numerous educational, professional and institutional contexts at the national level.

In addition, in the context of the necessary rethinking of teaching and assessment at universities dictated by the epidemiological emergency caused by Covid-19, teachers had to face unprecedented, emergency distance-teaching situations, which prompt reflections on professionalism, on the skills required to generate meaningful learning, and on possible forms of institutional support for online and blended teaching.

www.asduni.it

titleEn: Information session on the Japan Society for Promotion of Science (JSPS)
excerptEn: For those interested in funding and research career development in Japan The Japan Society for Promotion of Science (JSPS) is Japan’s most important agency for the funding and promotion of science. Through its various calls, the JSPS funds and promotes scientific talent and international scientific and training exchanges, among other functions. With the…
contentEn:
For those interested in funding and research career development in Japan 

The Japan Society for Promotion of Science (JSPS) is Japan’s most important agency for the funding and promotion of science. Through its various calls, the JSPS funds and promotes scientific talent and international scientific and training exchanges, among other functions. 

With the aim of strengthening collaboration between centres and researchers in Spain and Japan, the heads of the JSPS in Europe will be giving a talk at the Hispano-Japanese Cultural Centre of the University of Salamanca (Plaza de San Boal) on Wednesday 19 February at 19:00. The meeting will be attended by Prof. Saigo, director of the JSPS office in Strasbourg, and Mr Shigyo, a member of the same office. Dr Meziani and Dr Jiménez Espejo (CSIC) will also speak about their research experience in Japan.

This talk is aimed at students of any degree, researchers, lecturers and managers interested in collaborating with researchers in Japan or in developing part of their research career at Japanese institutions.

titleEn: César Gómez’s stay at the IUCE comes to an end
excerptEn: César Gómez is completing his master’s thesis at the Institute for Educational Research and Development (IIDE) of the Autonomous University of Baja California (Mexico). His stay ran from 7 September until last Friday, 29 November, at the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of…
contentEn: César Gómez is completing his master’s thesis at the Institute for Educational Research and Development (IIDE) of the Autonomous University of Baja California (Mexico). His stay at the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca ran from 7 September until last Friday, 29 November, to get to know the statistical models being applied to assess school effectiveness using data from the PISA tests (Secondary Education), and he has replicated them with data from Mexico.  He will continue working in the field of school effectiveness and the study of teaching and learning methodologies and strategies.

titleEn: 1st International Conference of Research in Education in Barcelona
excerptEn: On 4 and 5 November, Barcelona will host the 1st International Conference of Research in Education – “Education 2019: challenges, trends and commitments” (IRED’19). The Conference aims to analyse the immediate future of education along the lines set out in the report of the Institute of Education Research (IRE) of the University of Barcelona,…
contentEn:
On 4 and 5 November, Barcelona will host the 1st International Conference of Research in Education – “Education 2019: challenges, trends and commitments” (IRED’19). The Conference aims to analyse the immediate future of education along the lines set out in the report of the Institute of Education Research (IRE) of the University of Barcelona, published in 2018.

The official languages of the conference are Spanish, Catalan, English and Portuguese. Contributions and participation will therefore be accepted in these four languages. The Organising Committee reserves the right to arrange sessions grouped by language.

Thematic structure

The contents of the discussion and the presentation of reflections are organised around these three strands:

  • Challenges facing the education system over the coming years.
  • Trends: where current institutional policies are heading.
  • Commitments to be reached over the coming years.

Session structure

The two days of the conference are divided into three sessions:

  • Research and society
  • Research groups
  • Research institutes

More information available at http://www.ub.edu/ired19/

titleEn: Call for papers: EVALtrends2020 International Conference
excerptEn: EVALtrends2020 International Conference: Assessment as learning in the university: involving students in assessment practices, to be held from 25 to 27 February 2020 at the University of Cádiz (Spain). EVALtrends2020, centred on the topic Assessment as learning in the university: involving students in assessment practices, aspires…
contentEn:
EVALtrends2020 International Conference: Assessment as learning in the university: involving students in assessment practices, to be held from 25 to 27 February 2020 at the University of Cádiz (Spain).

EVALtrends2020, centred on the topic Assessment as learning in the university: involving students in assessment practices, aspires to establish a framework for debate on research results, innovations, experiences, practices and strategies around three thematic strands for reflection on assessment in higher education: learning through assessment, assessment of learning outcomes, and new spaces, new challenges: assessment in the hyper-classroom.
The contributions will be made through a summary (resumen) and an abstract, and may be of two types: research or good practices in assessment.

Given the objectives and structure of the conference, the maximum number of attendees is limited to 150 delegates. In the acceptance of registrations, preference will be given to teams of lecturers (4 authors maximum) who have submitted their contribution and obtained a positive assessment of it, so that the contribution reflects work carried out as a team and opens up the possibility of sharing research methods and results with other teams of researchers.

The deadline for submitting contributions is 1 November 2019 (Abstracts will be published in a book of proceedings with ISBN.).

We encourage you to take part and to create a space for fruitful exchange that helps define the challenges we must face through assessment, on the way towards assessment 2030.

More detailed information available at: evaltrends2020.uca.es

The full call for papers can be downloaded at:

Llamada-EVALtrends2020Download

titleEn: Institutes session at IRED’19 – «Education 2019: challenges, trends and commitments»
excerptEn: Of interest to the members of the Institute’s Groups and to those who would like to take part in an institute: 1st International Conference of Research in Education «Education 2019: challenges, trends and commitments» IRED’19 Barcelona, 4-5 November 2019 http://www.ub.edu/ired19/ Institute of Education Research, University of Barcelona Spanish Network of Education Research Ins…
contentEn:
Of interest to the members of the Institute’s Groups and to those who would like to take part in an institute:

1st International Conference of Research in Education

«Education 2019: challenges, trends and commitments»

IRED’19

Barcelona, 4-5 November 2019

http://www.ub.edu/ired19/

Institute of Education Research, University of Barcelona

Spanish Network of Education Research Institutes

Objective

To analyse the immediate future of education along the lines set out in the report of the Institute of Education Research (IRE) of the University of Barcelona, published in 2018.

Thematic structure

The contents of the discussion and the presentation of reflections are organised around these three strands:

  • Challenges facing the education system over the coming years.
  • Trends towards which current institutional policies are heading.
  • Commitments to be reached over the coming years.

Structure of the sessions

The two days of the conference are divided into three sessions:

  • Papers: Research and society
  • Round tables: Research groups
  • Research institutes

Invited speakers

Dr Henry Giroux
Dr Boaventura de Sousa Santos

Registration

Reduced rate: until 15 July

Dates

> Submission

  • First option: until 15 June 2019.
  • Second option: until 31 July 2019.

> Evaluation

  • First option: until 1 July 2019.
  • Second option: until 1 October 2019.

Available to download in PDF in Spanish, English, Catalan and Portuguese
